Quelle: The Guardian / Council on Foreign Relations / World Economic Forum
Zusammenfassung: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are scheduled to meet in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026. The summit aims to stabilize relations strained by disputes over trade, Taiwan, and the ongoing war in Iran. Economic deliverables are expected to be prioritized, with potential for an extension of the trade truce and significant Chinese purchases of US goods. China's trade grew faster than expected in April, reversing a decline in exports to the United States, although year-to-date bilateral trade has decreased. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is invited to join the US delegation.

Quelle: Taipei Times, TrendForce, Gotrade, digitimes, TradingKey, GuruFocus, CMC Markets
Zusammenfassung: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported a 58% year-over-year net income jump in Q1 2026, driven by robust AI chip demand, with advanced nodes (7nm and below) constituting 74% of its wafer revenue. The company's 2nm node is slated for volume production in the second half of 2026, with significant capacity already booked by major clients like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia. Additionally, TSMC has signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding with Sony Semiconductor Solutions Corp to establish a joint venture for the development and production of next-generation image sensors in Kumamoto, Japan. These developments, alongside strong performance from other semiconductor-related companies like Samsung Electronics, have propelled the Japanese Nikkei 225 and South Korean KOSPI indices to record highs, reflecting a positive market sentiment fueled by AI euphoria and strong tech sector growth in Asia.
